headline: Amazon to slap a 3.5% surcharge on third-party sellers as Iran war drives up fuel prices
summary: Amazon is slapping a 3.5% fuel and logistics surcharge on third-party sellers using its platform starting later this month amid a spike in fuel prices since the war in Iran started.  The temporary charge is effective April 17 for many of the sellers who use Amazon’s fulfillment services, the online behemoth confirmed to The Associated Press in an email Thursday.  “Elevated costs in fuel and logistics have increased the cost of operating across the industry,” Amazon said in the emailed statement.

content.title: Amazon to slap a 3.5% surcharge on third-party sellers as Iran war drives up fuel prices
content.summary: Amazon is slapping a 3.5% fuel and logistics surcharge on third-party sellers using its platform starting later this month amid a spike in fuel prices since the war in Iran started.  The temporary charge is effective April 17 for many of the sellers who use Amazon’s fulfillment services, the online behemoth confirmed to The Associated Press in an email Thursday.  “Elevated costs in fuel and logistics have increased the cost of operating across the industry,” Amazon said in the emailed statement.

headline: Apple At 50 Weighs AI Partnerships Satellite Ties And Succession
summary: Apple (NasdaqGS:AAPL) marks its 50th anniversary as investors focus on CEO Tim Cook's eventual succession and the company's long term direction. Apple plans to open Siri to third party AI assistants on iOS, signaling a shift toward a more open approach to digital assistants. Amazon is reported to be in talks to acquire Globalstar, Apple's satellite partner for emergency and connectivity features. Apple enters this milestone moment with a current share price of $255.92 and multi year returns...

headline: How Investors May Respond To Mastercard (MA) Pivot Toward Digital Assets And Core Network Partnerships
summary: In recent weeks, Mastercard announced plans to sell its recently acquired Nets real-time payments unit, which generates about US$370 million in annual revenue, while also closing the acquisition of stablecoin platform BVNK and expanding its role as the network behind new Amazon–U.S. Bank small business credit cards. Together, the Nets divestment, BVNK deal and Amazon partnership highlight Mastercard’s pivot toward higher-margin digital asset infrastructure and services while doubling down on...

headline: Amazon Surcharge And Union Ruling Test Margins Versus Valuation Upside
summary: Amazon.com (NasdaqGS: AMZN) plans to apply a temporary 3.5% fuel and logistics surcharge on U.S. and Canada sellers, citing higher costs linked to the Iran conflict. The National Labor Relations Board has ordered Amazon to recognize and bargain with the union at its Staten Island warehouse. headline: Alphabet vs. Tesla: This stock is just an 'immense cash flow machine'
summary: Yahoo Finance's "Market Madness" tournament has whittled down the competition of top-performing companies, pairing Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL) against Tesla (TSLA) and Palantir Technologies (PLTR) against Walmart (WMT) in today's matchups.Carnegie Investment Counsel director of research Greg Halter decides who will move on to the Final Four in this clash of the corporations, detailing his reasoning and analysis behind each company's stock and growth drivers.

content.title: Alphabet vs. Tesla: This stock is just an 'immense cash flow machine'
content.description:
Yahoo Finance's "Market Madness" tournament has whittled down the competition of top-performing companies, pairing Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL) against Tesla (TSLA) and Palantir Technologies (PLTR) against Walmart (WMT) in today's matchups.

Carnegie Investment Counsel director of research Greg Halter decides who will move on to the Final Four in this clash of the corporations, detailing his reasoning and analysis behind each company's stock and growth drivers.

headline: 3 Internet Stocks Worth Your Attention
summary: Whether it be online shopping or social media, secular forces are propelling consumer internet businesses forward. But it’s not all sunshine and rainbows as consumer purchasing power can make or break demand. Unfortunately, the market seems to believe stormy skies are ahead as the industry has shed 30.3% over the past six months. This performance was much worse than the S&P 500’s 2.1% decline.
